channel.title: Group Work on Robotic Modelling & Drone Skin Design
channel.description: Recent content in Group Work on Robotic Modelling & Drone Skin Design

item.title: Building the Soapomorph
item.description:
While the CAD and STL files were completed, lack of availability of resources, such as the 3D Factory’s 3D printers and laser cutters, meant that not all components could be manufactured within the project’s timeframe.

Skull, Jaw and Tongue

As a substitute for the topmost, 3D printed components, an animation was produced in Blender to simulate the movement of the jaw and tongue, shown in Figure 1.

Due to time and experience constraints, the jaw and tongue do not move simultaneously like the actual mechanism inherently would, but it provides a useful demonstration of the mechanism’s movement and the detachable lid. It also provides a preview of the visibility of the join, where the separately printed back and front of the skull meet.

item.title: Designing the Soapomorph
item.description:
Stakeholders

A stakeholder is an individual, group or organisation that has an interest in a product or service, can influence its decisions, or is affected by its outcomes and/or deliverables (Stakeholder | Glossary, no date).

The stakeholders requiring consideration when developing the Soapomorph are detailed in the table below.

Stakeholder Interest or Influence Action
Bathroom cleaner Needs to clean and refill it safely and efficiently Accessible hatch for refilling and easily cleanable surfaces
Guests Needs to be able to safely and easily interact with the mechanism Zero-contact motion sensor, non-toxic surfaces, properly secured in place
Venue owners Needs return on investment, safety for guests, easy disposal Price of manufacture is fair relative to cost, disposable, sustainable materials, as above
Everyone Needs environment to not be polluted Sustainable materials, convenient to dispose of properly

Specifications

The device is a soap dispenser with a loosely-Xenomorph-inspired design. It should have no toxic or sharp features on the surfaces. It should be easy to clean and dispose of at its end of life. The skull component that touch the soap should be at least somewhat resistant to water and weak alkalise such as soap.

item.title: Attending Workshops and Exploring Technologies
item.description:
A series of workshops were organised to facilitate the group’s exposure to the technologies and tools required to complete the Soapomorph project.

3D Modelling

The workshops provided the group with opportunities for hands-on experience with two 3D modelling software packages: Autodesk Fusion and Blender, each possessing advantages and drawbacks depending on the use case.

item.title: Group Forming and Project Brainstorming
item.description:
Group Formation

A group of people was formed from the cohort of students studying the Robotic Modelling and Drone Skin Design module as part of the AI & Robotics Bachelor’s degree, for the purpose of designing, manufacturing and assembling a product suitable for demonstrating the following technologies:

  • 3D scanning hardware and software
  • Motion capture hardware and software
  • Computer Aided Design (CAD) software
  • 3D modelling software
  • Haptic input hardware paired with associated 3D sculpting software
  • 3D printing hardware
  • Laser cutting hardware
  • Resin production and postproduction

A well-balanced group emerged from the formation process, with a varied combination of specialities, including proficiency at 3D modelling with Blender, ability to create both 2D and 3D designs using CAD software, skill in woodworking, and extensive experience with relevant electronic components such as servo motors and the allowances, required to house and connect them.
